LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENTS DENTAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA AND DEPARTMENT OF CONSUMER AFFAIRS' MOTION TO STRIKE AND/OR TAX COSTS IS DENIED. PER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 1032(A)(4), PETITIONER IS THE PREVAILING PARTY BY HAVING OBTAINED A WRIT OF MANDATE IN HIS FAVOR WHICH RESULTED IN A LESSER DISCIPLINE AND COSTS ARE AWARDED TO HIM AS AN EXERCISE OF THE COURT'S DISCRETION. PETITIONER'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT WHICH MAY BE SENT VIA E-MAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER MARK MEDINNUS' MOTION TO FIX ATTORNEYS FEES UNDER GOVERNMENT CODE 800(A), PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS DENIED. RESPONDENT'S PRIOR DECISION WHICH WAS CHALLENGED BY PETITIONER WAS NOT ARBITRARY OR CAPRICIOUS. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT WHICH MAY BE SENT VIA E-MAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike And/Or Tax Costs Of Petitioner Medinnus
Off Calendar Mar-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ROBERT W. STEWART, ESQ., Tel No. 415-479-3600 appearing for the Petitioner;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. RESPONDENTS DENTAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA AND DEPARTMENT OF CONSUMER AFFAIRS' Motion To Strike And/Or Tax Costs is denied. Per Code of Civil Procedure 1032(a)(4), petitioner is the prevailing party by having obtained a writ of mandate in his favor which resulted in a lesser discipline and costs are awarded to him as an exercise of the court's discretion. Petitioner's counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court which may be sent via e-mail to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion To Fix Attorneys Fees Under Government Code 800(A)
Off Calendar Mar-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ROBERT W. STEWART, ESQ., Tel No. 415-479-3600 appearing for the Petitioner;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to PETITIONER MARK MEDINNUS' Motion To Fix Attorneys Fees Under Government Code 800(A), Petitioner's motion for attorney's fees is denied. Respondent's prior decision which was challenged by petitioner was not arbitrary or capricious. The prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court which may be sent via e-mail to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Petitioner Mark Medinnus Motion Re Hearing Of Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Feb-11-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioner Mark Medinnus' Motion Re Hearing Of Writ Of Mandamus is submitted. No appearances were required at the hearing. The Court will issue an Order within the next few days. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Petitioner Mark Medinnus Motion Re Hearing Of Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Dec-31-2014 Continued To Jan-20-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ROBERT W. STEWART, Tel No. (415) 479-3600 appearing for the Petitioner Mark Grant Medinnus;
Deputy Attorney General MARETTA WARD of the Office of the Attorney General, Tel. (415) 703-1384 appearing for the Respondent Dental Board of California, Department of Consumer Affairs, State of California.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court ruled as follows regarding PETITIONER MARK MEDINNUS' Hearing on Writ Of Mandamus: The Court shall send this matter back to the Dental Board of California with a statement that the Petitioner may have been disciplined based on drug use during the relevant probationary period of time. It appears to the Court that the Petitioner's penalty reflected as much. The Court finds that the Petitioner was contrite in admitting the charges against him. It appears to the Court that there was no evidence of the Petitioner using drugs. The Dental Board should reconsider the Petitioner's penalty, and it is the Court's recommendation that the Petitioner be placed on continued supervised probation for an extended period of time (such as four years). The Petitioner should be allowed to present evidence of rehabilitation regarding drug use (from 2002 onward). The Court makes no finding here that Petitioner was or was not involved with drugs. Mr. STEWART SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. The court shall set a control hearing date in this matter for January 30, 2015 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302. If there is a signed order prior to 1/30/15, parties need not appear on 1/30/15. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)
